pages.title: Electric Heater Running Cost Calculator UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Use the UK electric heater running cost calculator, with 2kW heater examples, monthly cost tables and current Ofgem-backed electricity assumptions.

relatedRoutes.text: Use this when you want the quick 2kW answer before adjusting your own runtime.

faq.question: How much does a 2kW heater cost per hour in the UK?
faq.answer: At 24.67p per kWh, a 2kW heater costs about 49p per hour at full power. If it cycles down after the room warms up, the average cost can be lower.

faq.question: How much does electric heating cost per month?
faq.answer: A 2kW heater used four hours a day at full power costs about GBP 59.21 over a 30-day month at 24.67p per kWh. Regular whole-room electric heating can therefore become expensive quickly.

faq.question: Do oil radiators, fan heaters and panel heaters cost different amounts to run?
faq.answer: At the same wattage and runtime, direct electric heaters cost the same per kWh. The practical difference is warm-up speed, comfort, controls and how long the heater stays near full output.

pages.title: 2kW Heater Cost Per Hour UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: See what a 2kW heater costs per hour, evening, week and month in the UK using the current Ofgem electricity benchmark.
pages.h1: 2kW heater cost per hour in the UK
pages.lead: A 2kW heater is one of the most common UK portable heater sizes, so it is the fastest way to understand how direct electric room heat affects a bill.
pages.summary: At the current 24.67p/kWh benchmark, a 2kW heater costs about 49p for one hour at full power.
pages.angle: This page is best for the exact hourly answer. Use the main calculator when you want to adjust duty cycle, days per week or your own tariff.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Adjust wattage, duty cycle and weekly use.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heating cost per month
relatedRoutes.text: See what regular use does to a monthly bill.

relatedRoutes.title: Oil radiator vs fan heater
relatedRoutes.text: Compare the practical heater choice after the cost baseline.

faq.question: Is a 2kW heater expensive to run?
faq.answer: It can be. One hour is about 49p at 24.67p/kWh, but four hours every day is about GBP 59 over a 30-day month at full power.

faq.question: Does a 2kW oil radiator cost less than a 2kW fan heater?
faq.answer: Not at full power. Both use 2kWh per hour at full output. The real difference is how they heat the room and how often their thermostat cycles.

pages.title: Electric Heating Cost Per Month UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate electric heating cost per month in the UK, with 1kW, 1.5kW, 2kW and 2.5kW examples using current electricity rates.
pages.lead: Monthly electric heating cost depends on wattage, hours and how much the heater cycles down once the room is warm.
pages.summary: A 2kW heater used four hours a day at full power costs about GBP 59.21 over 30 days at 24.67p/kWh.
pages.angle: This page is best when you are trying to estimate the winter bill effect of regular room heating.

relatedRoutes.title: Is electric heating expensive?

faq.question: How do I calculate monthly electric heating cost?
faq.answer: Multiply watts divided by 1000 by hours used, days used and electricity price per kWh. For a 2kW heater used four hours for 30 days at 24.67p/kWh, the monthly cost is about GBP 59.21.

faq.question: Why can the real monthly cost be lower than the full-power table?
faq.answer: A thermostat-controlled heater may cycle down once the room is warm. Poorly insulated or oversized spaces can keep the heater closer to full power for longer.

pages.title: Fan Heater Running Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate fan heater running costs in the UK, including 2kW examples and when fast warm-up becomes expensive.
pages.lead: Fan heaters warm a person or small room quickly, but common 2kW models can become expensive if they run for long sessions.
pages.summary: A 2kW fan heater costs about 49p per hour at full power using 24.67p/kWh electricity.
pages.angle: Best for quick bursts and short warm-up periods; less attractive for long steady heating in a poor-fit room.

relatedRoutes.title: Oil radiator vs fan heater
relatedRoutes.text: Use this when comfort and warm-up speed matter.

relatedRoutes.title: 2kW heater cost per hour
relatedRoutes.text: See the full-power 2kW benchmark.

relatedRoutes.title: Heater room size calculator
relatedRoutes.text: Check whether the room needs more heat than a fan heater can sensibly provide.

faq.question: Are fan heaters cheap to run?
faq.answer: They can be cheap for short bursts, but a common 2kW fan heater costs about 49p per hour at full power.

faq.question: Why does a fan heater feel expensive?
faq.answer: It often heats quickly but loses comfort quickly after switching off, so people may run it for longer or more often than expected.

pages.title: Oil-Filled Radiator Running Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate oil-filled radiator running costs in the UK and compare full-power cost with real thermostat cycling.
pages.lead: Oil-filled radiators are still direct electric heaters, but their slower, steadier feel can change how people use them.
pages.summary: A 1.5kW oil-filled radiator costs about 37p per hour at full power using 24.67p/kWh electricity.
pages.angle: The cost advantage usually comes from thermostat cycling and comfort pattern, not from a different price per kWh.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Adjust wattage and duty cycle.

relatedRoutes.title: Panel heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Compare with another steady electric heater.

faq.question: Is an oil-filled radiator cheaper than a fan heater?
faq.answer: At the same wattage and runtime, no. It may feel more economical if the thermostat cycles down and the room holds heat well.

faq.question: Does the oil inside reduce electricity use?
faq.answer: The oil stores and releases heat, but the electricity used still depends on wattage, runtime and thermostat behaviour.

pages.title: Panel Heater Running Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate panel heater running costs in the UK, with hourly and monthly examples for common wattages.
pages.lead: Panel heaters are simple direct electric room heaters. Their bill impact depends heavily on wattage, hours and controls.
pages.summary: A 1.5kW panel heater costs about 37p per hour at full power using 24.67p/kWh electricity.
pages.angle: Best judged as a controlled room-heating option, not as a special low-cost form of electricity.

relatedRoutes.title: Panel heater vs oil radiator
relatedRoutes.text: Compare two steadier electric heating options.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Model panel heater duty cycle and schedule.

relatedRoutes.title: Is electric heating expensive?
relatedRoutes.text: Understand the wider electric-heating verdict.

faq.question: Are panel heaters expensive to run?
faq.answer: They can be if used as regular room heating for long hours. A 1.5kW panel heater is about 37p per hour at full power on the current benchmark.

faq.question: Do smart controls make panel heaters cheaper?
faq.answer: Controls can reduce wasted runtime, but they do not change the underlying cost per kWh.

pages.title: Infrared Heater Running Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate infrared heater running costs in the UK and understand when lower wattage helps or does not help.
pages.lead: Infrared heaters often use lower wattages than fan heaters, but the value depends on whether they warm the person or zone you actually care about.
pages.summary: An 800W infrared heater costs about 20p per hour at full power using 24.67p/kWh electricity.
pages.angle: Infrared can look cheaper when it targets a person or zone instead of trying to heat a whole cold room.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Compare your own wattage and schedule.

relatedRoutes.title: Heating option comparison
relatedRoutes.text: Check whether infrared suits the room use.

relatedRoutes.title: Heater room size calculator
relatedRoutes.text: Check whether you need room heat or targeted comfort.

faq.question: Are infrared heaters cheaper to run?
faq.answer: They can be cheaper per hour if the wattage is lower, but only if the targeted heat solves the comfort problem.

faq.question: Can an infrared heater replace room heating?
faq.answer: Sometimes for spot heating, but it may not be enough if the whole room needs to be warm.

pages.title: Electric Boiler Running Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Estimate electric boiler running costs in the UK and why whole-home direct electric heating can become expensive.
pages.lead: Electric boilers can be simple to install in some homes, but they use electricity for whole-home heat, so the monthly cost can climb quickly.
pages.summary: A 9kW electric boiler uses 9kWh in one hour at full output, costing about GBP 2.22 at 24.67p/kWh.
pages.angle: This page is a high-level cost sense-check, not an installer heat-loss design or tariff quote.

relatedRoutes.title: Methodology
relatedRoutes.text: Review how Home Energy Scout handles estimate limits.

faq.question: Why can an electric boiler be expensive to run?
faq.answer: It is using electricity for whole-home heat. Electricity unit prices are much higher than gas unit prices in the current Ofgem benchmark.

faq.question: Is this an installation cost estimate?
faq.answer: No. This page focuses on running-cost logic and should not replace quotes or a proper heat-loss assessment.

pages.title: Is Electric Heating Expensive in the UK? | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Understand when electric heating is expensive in the UK, when it can still make sense, and how current electricity and gas rates change the answer.
pages.h1: Is electric heating expensive in the UK?
pages.lead: Electric heating is not automatically wrong, but direct electric heat is easy to make expensive when it is used for long hours or whole-home heating.
pages.summary: For whole-room regular heating, direct electric heating is usually expensive compared with mains gas because the electricity unit rate is much higher.
pages.angle: Electric heat can still make sense for short bursts, rooms without central heating, targeted comfort, or homes where installation simplicity matters.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Put the verdict against your own use pattern.

relatedRoutes.title: Gas vs electric heating
relatedRoutes.text: Check whole-home heating economics.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heating cost per month
relatedRoutes.text: Translate hourly cost into bill impact.

faq.question: Is electric heating always expensive?
faq.answer: No. It can be sensible for short, targeted use. It becomes expensive when direct electric heat is used for large areas or long hours.

faq.question: Why is electric heating often dearer than gas?
faq.answer: The current benchmark electricity unit rate is much higher than the gas unit rate, so direct electric heat has a higher fuel cost per kWh.

pages.title: Electric Heater vs Central Heating Cost UK | Home Energy Scout
pages.description: Compare electric heater and central heating cost logic in the UK, including when single-room electric heat can still make sense.
pages.h1: Electric heater vs central heating cost
pages.lead: The cheaper option depends on whether you need one room warm for a short time or a whole home warm for longer.
pages.summary: A plug-in electric heater can make sense for short single-room use, but central heating often wins when several rooms need heat.
pages.angle: Use this as decision logic before modelling your exact heater or whole-home setup.

relatedRoutes.title: Gas vs electric heating
relatedRoutes.text: Compare fuels at the system level.

relatedRoutes.title: Electric heater running cost
relatedRoutes.text: Model the plug-in heater case.

relatedRoutes.title: Heater room size calculator
relatedRoutes.text: Check whether a plug-in heater is realistic for the room.

faq.question: Is it cheaper to use an electric heater for one room?
faq.answer: Sometimes, especially for short use when you do not need the whole home warm. For longer sessions or multiple rooms, central heating may be cheaper overall.

faq.question: Should I turn off central heating and use plug-in heaters?
faq.answer: Not as a blanket rule. Model the room, hours and comfort need first, because direct electric heat can become expensive quickly.
